rules.statement: Use plan mode before building a large feature to produce a comprehensive implementation plan first
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Large features are built without a roadmap, leading to missed dependencies, incorrect sequencing, and multiple costly rework cycles as the agent discovers requirements mid-implementation

rules.statement: Test API integrations early and in isolation before building UI on top of them
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: UI is built on assumptions about API response format that turn out to be incorrect, requiring costly rework of both API handling and UI layers simultaneously

rules.statement: Always review every Git change after agent edits because the agent frequently gets confused
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Agent introduces unintended deletions, overwrites, or incorrect logic that goes undetected until it compounds into harder-to-diagnose bugs in later development stages

rules.statement: Be specific with the agent about data formats (JSON structure, field names) or it will improvise
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Agent generates mismatched data structures that do not align with database schemas or API contracts, causing runtime errors, broken UI bindings, and integration failures

rules.statement: When implementing multiple phases, require the agent to mark phases done and get approval before proceeding
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: Agent races ahead through multiple phases, compounding errors from earlier phases into later ones, making debugging exponentially harder and requiring rollback of multiple completed phases

rules.statement: Always match database field names exactly between application code and UI components
rules.failure_mode_if_violated: UI components bind to non-existent or misspelled field names, resulting in blank displays, undefined errors, or silent data loss where user input is written to wrong columns

heuristics.condition: IF database and API setup is done
heuristics.action: THEN UI implementation is usually easy for the AI
heuristics.reason: Once data contracts are established and APIs are verified, the AI can generate UI components confidently because the input/output shapes are known and the remaining work is presentational

heuristics.condition: IF implementing many things at once
heuristics.action: THEN issues compound; do phases individually with testing
heuristics.reason: Multiple simultaneous UI and logic changes create combinatorial failure modes that are extremely difficult to debug; sequential implementation with testing gates isolates failures to a single phase

heuristics.condition: IF the API returns a CORS error from the browser
heuristics.action: THEN create a server-side API route instead
heuristics.reason: Browser-based API calls are blocked by CORS policies on most third-party APIs; routing through a server-side endpoint (e.g., Next.js API route) bypasses this restriction entirely

heuristics.condition: IF a feature is large (multiple components, API calls, state management)
heuristics.action: THEN use plan mode first to increase chances of one-shotting it
heuristics.reason: Planning upfront identifies component hierarchy, data flow, state dependencies, and potential issues before code generation begins, reducing the number of iterations needed

frameworks.name: SaaS Feature Development Workflow
steps: Sketch the feature concept on paper or whiteboard
Plan the feature with a virtual CTO persona for architecture review
Enter plan mode in Claude Code to generate a comprehensive implementation plan
Review the plan for completeness, correct sequencing, and feasibility
Execute the plan phase by phase with the agent
Test each completed phase in the browser
Debug any issues found during testing before proceeding

frameworks.name: Phased Development with Testing Gates
steps: Define numbered phases with clear deliverables in a to-do file
Agent completes all steps within a single phase
Agent marks the phase as done with a summary of changes
Human reviews the completed phase in the browser and code
Human approves or requests revisions before the next phase
Agent proceeds to the next phase only after explicit approval
Repeat until all phases are complete and verified

frameworks.name: API Integration Testing Pattern
steps: Add a temporary test button to the UI
Wire the button to trigger the target API call
Check the browser console and network tab for response data
Verify the response structure matches expected format
Debug any issues with the API call, CORS, or response parsing
Remove the test button once integration is verified
Proceed with building the production UI on top of the verified API

frameworks.name: SaaS MVP Build Pipeline
steps: Data ingestion via N8N workflows or direct API connections
Build messaging UI for user interaction and input
Integrate external APIs (Perplexity, OpenAI, etc.)
Parse and display API responses in the UI with proper formatting
Generate reports and dashboards from processed data
UI polish, responsive design, and user experience refinement

frameworks.name: Scrappy-to-Production Stack
steps: Validate the idea with N8N + Google Sheets for rapid no-code prototyping
Build a structured prototype with Lovable + Supabase for visual MVP
Graduate to Cursor/IDE-based development for full production readiness
Integrate payments, auth, and production infrastructure
Ship and iterate based on user feedback

tech_patterns.name: Stack
tech_patterns.description: Core development stack for frontend/UX vibe-coding combining Cursor IDE for AI-assisted editing, Claude Code terminal for agentic development, Stripe for payments, plan mode for architecture, GitHub for version control, and Manis for configuration management

tech_patterns.name: IDEs
tech_patterns.description: The three primary AI-augmented IDEs for vibe-coding SaaS products: VS Code (free, open-source foundation), Cursor (AI-first fork of VS Code with integrated chat and autocomplete), and Windsurf (AI-enhanced VS Code fork with flow-based editing). All are free and share the VS Code extension ecosystem

tech_patterns.name: LinkBerry Example
tech_patterns.description: Reference SaaS application for LinkedIn content management featuring interview-based content extraction, AI-generated voice profiles, automated post draft generation, and engagement pod coordination for distribution amplification

tech_patterns.name: UGC Video Pipeline
tech_patterns.description: Automated user-generated content video creation pipeline that takes an Amazon product link, crawls the product page for details and images, extracts key selling points and specifications, feeds the extracted data to a video generation model, and outputs a polished product video ready for social media

metrics.name: Full SaaS MVP built in a single session
Typical feature build time with Claude Code
